scenario_title: Add RR beneath an existing CNAME alias
test_rationale: This test sets up a name that is already in the CNAME-only state and then performs a single syntactically valid UPDATE that adds an A RR at that same owner name. The protocol gap is whether the server must reject the update, and if so with which code, or whether it may accept/transform the change. The follow-up QUERYs check both the CNAME rrset and the A rrset so the runner can observe whether the zone remained unchanged or the conflicting data was applied.

group_1.behavior: Reject the UPDATE as a semantic conflict with the existing CNAME, commonly with YXRRSET or REFUSED, leaving the CNAME-only state unchanged.
group_1.post_state: alias.campus.edu. continues to answer with the original CNAME rrset; no A rrset is present.

group_2.behavior: Reject the UPDATE with REFUSED, or otherwise not apply the conflicting add; the CNAME-only state remains unchanged.
group_2.post_state: alias.campus.edu. continues to answer with the original CNAME rrset; no A rrset is present.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1/§10.1.1 requires CNAME exclusivity, but RFC 2136 §3.4.2 does not specify the UPDATE-time RCODE or whether an attempted add_rr of a non-CNAME type at an existing CNAME owner must be atomically rejected versus handled in some server-defined way. That leaves the failure mode and exact response code underspecified.
